strategies.hypothesis: A single independent answer is the minimum-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Direct One

strategies.hypothesis: A small independent plurality is a stable low-cost baseline.
strategies.name: Five-Vote Plurality

strategies.hypothesis: Independent repairers can escape a candidate set whose correct answer is absent or underrepresented.
strategies.name: Five Solvers plus Three Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: Replicating the strongest judge-free control on a fresh panel will determine whether its improved constraint performance and 10/12 candidate oracle were signal or small-sample noise.
strategies.name: Fifteen-Vote Plurality Replication

strategies.hypothesis: Three independent repairs added to seven base votes can overturn fragmented wrong pluralities while preserving base-supported answers; skipping review under a strict majority avoids calls in cases that three added votes generally cannot reverse.
strategies.name: Seven Solvers plus Three Conservative Repairers

strategies.hypothesis: A broader bank should improve candidate coverage, while frequency-aware falsification and combined voting can promote a verified minority candidate without giving reviewers unilateral power to reverse the base.
strategies.name: Eleven Solvers plus Three Consensus Falsifiers
